task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface) and verify that a layer named 'COMPASS_ROSE' exists and is active.

task_completion.description: Draw outer and inner circles centered at the origin
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing canvas for two concentric circles. The outer circle should be significantly larger (radius 100) than the inner circle (radius 20).
Look for a vertical line and a horizontal line intersecting at the center of the circles. These lines should extend exactly to the edge of the outer circle (representing 100 units).

task_completion.description: Draw four ordinal direction lines at 45-degree angles
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for diagonal lines forming an 'X' intersecting at the center. These lines should be noticeably shorter than the cardinal lines, ending before they reach the outer circle (representing 70 units).

task_completion.description: Add a text label 'N' at the top
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the letter 'N' placed just above the top vertical line (North cardinal line) in the drawing.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing as a DXF file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD window title bar for 'compass_rose.dxf' or check the command line history at the bottom of the screen for a message indicating the file was saved to '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/compass_rose.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window should be visible with its standard toolbars, menus, and drawing canvas being actively used.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal script to bypass GUI drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows visible where the agent is running Python or bash scripts to programmatically generate the DXF file.

integrity.description: The drawing is displayed in LibreCAD, not an image viewer
integrity.visual_evidence: The compass rose must be seen within the LibreCAD workspace, not opened as a pre-existing image in a generic image viewer or web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires drawing in LibreCAD. Outer circle radius is 100, inner circle radius is 20. Cardinal lines (N, S, E, W) extend 100 units from the center, meaning they should exactly touch the outer circle. Ordinal lines (NE, NW, SE, SW) extend 70 units, meaning they should stop short of the outer circle. The text 'N' must be at approximately (0, 110), just above the North line. The layer must be named 'COMPASS_ROSE'. The file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/compass_rose.dxf'. A geometric tolerance of 3.0 units is acceptable.
